name: Street Intervention Before–After Preferences (GSV Time Travel)
description: Visual stated-preference template from Lopes, Valença & Moura (Landscape and Urban Planning, 2026): eight before/after pairwise street-intervention comparisons (car / bus / active modes) plus characterization items from Table 1. Paper used GSV Time Machine iframes; this template uses fixed 2-image sets per trial (upload before/after pairs into set folders).
author: Lopes, Valença & Moura

config.title: Street Intervention Preferences (Before–After GSV) — Lopes et al., 2026
config.description: Based on: Lopes, M. I., Valença, G., & Moura, F. (2026). Exploring Before-and-After visual preferences of street interventions using Google Street View time travel. Landscape and Urban Planning, 273, 105686. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.landurbplan.2026.105686. Eight pairwise comparisons (2 car, 2 bus, 4 active-modes) plus demographics/travel behaviour (Table 1). Preference and familiarity wording are verbatim from the article.

elements.name: overview_html
elements.html:
Lopes, Valença & Moura (2026) — Before-and-after visual preferences of street interventions using Google Street View time travel. Landscape and Urban Planning, 273, 105686. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.landurbplan.2026.105686

This template follows the paper’s LimeSurvey design (~39 items, ~10 min): 8 pairwise before/after preference trials (2 car + 2 bus + 4 active-modes) plus a participant characterization section (Table 1).

  • Preference wording (verbatim): Please select the urban environment you prefer.
  • Familiarity (verbatim): Do you know this place?
  • Paper method: embed GSV Time Machine iframes (Google ToS–compliant). This platform alternatively uses fixed 2-image sets per trial folder.

Participation in the original study was voluntary with no monetary compensation.

elements.name: instructions_html
elements.html:
You will see eight street interventions. For each one, compare the urban environment before and after the intervention.

In the original study, respondents were instructed to navigate the Street View imagery to explore the street in both pre- and post-intervention states (full 360° view).

Intervention categories (paper definitions):

  1. Car-oriented — e.g. converting cycle lanes into traffic/parking, reducing sidewalk for parking, expanding roadways.
  2. Bus-oriented — e.g. exclusive bus lanes, banning private cars for buses only.
  3. Active-modes-oriented — e.g. shared spaces, car-free zones, cycle lanes, widened sidewalks.

elements.name: trial_car_1_instructions
elements.html:
Car-oriented intervention

Explore the street before and after the intervention (paper: GSV Time Machine iframes; here: the two images in the set below), then answer.

Set folder: car-1 (exactly 2 files: before + after).

elements.name: trial_car_1_preference
elements.title: Please select the urban environment you prefer.
elements.description: Paper DV (verbatim). Forced choice, no ties. Coding in the article: Y = 1 if after (post-intervention) preferred, Y = 0 if before preferred. Upload before.jpg + after.jpg to set folder `car-1` (exactly 2 files).

elements.name: trial_car_1_familiarity
elements.title: Do you know this place?
elements.description: Familiarity (binary; Table 1).

pages.name: characterization
pages.title: About you
pages.description: Participants’ Characterization Section (15 items; Table 1). Only constructs listed in the article.

elements.name: age
elements.title: Age
elements.description: Socio-demographics — Age (ordinal in Table 1; enter years).

elements.name: gender
elements.title: Gender
elements.description: Socio-demographics — Gender (categorical). Sample reported male / female / other.

elements.name: having_children
elements.title: Do you have children?
elements.description: Having Children (binary; Table 1).

elements.name: number_of_children
elements.title: Number of children
elements.description: Number of Children (ordinal; Table 1).

elements.name: age_of_children
elements.title: Age of children
elements.description: Age of Children (categorical; Table 1). Exact response options not listed in the article — free text.

elements.name: having_pets_dogs
elements.title: Do you have pets/dogs?
elements.description: Having Pets/Dogs (binary; Table 1).

elements.name: disability
elements.title: Disability
elements.description: Disability (categorical; Table 1). Exact options not listed — free text / self-report.

elements.name: occupation
elements.title: Occupation
elements.description: Occupation (categorical; Table 1). Exact options not listed — free text.

elements.name: city_of_residence
elements.title: City of residence
elements.description: Residence & nationality — City of Residence (Table 1).

elements.name: country_of_residence
elements.title: Country of residence
elements.description: Country of Residence (Table 1).

elements.name: nationality
elements.title: Nationality
elements.description: Nationality (Table 1).

elements.name: urban_planning_specialist
elements.title: Do you have a background / specialization in urban planning (or related transport planning)?
elements.description: Urban Planning Background — Specialist (binary; Table 1).

elements.name: role_type
elements.title: Role type (if specialist)
elements.description: Role Type (categorical; Table 1). Sample was mainly academics within urban planning — free text.

elements.name: commute_modes
elements.title: What mode(s) do you use for your commute?
elements.description: Travel Behaviour — Commute Modes (categorical; Table 1). Analysis used combinations (car, active, PT, etc.).

elements.name: commute_time
elements.title: Commute time
elements.description: Commute Time (ordinal; Table 1). Exact bins not listed — enter typical one-way minutes.

elements.name: car_ownership
elements.title: Do you own a car?
elements.description: Car Ownership (binary; Table 1).

elements.name: workplace_home_parking
elements.title: Workplace / home parking
elements.description: Workplace/Home Parking (categorical; Table 1). Exact options not listed — free text.
